mnemonics: THIS object is ME (My Execution) - Use 'this' to refer to the current object instance.
keyPoints: 'this' refers to the current instance of the class.
Used to distinguish between instance variables and parameters with the same name.
Can be used to call one constructor from another constructor in the same class.
Used to pass the current object as a parameter to other methods.
commonMistakes: Using 'this' unnecessarily when there's no naming conflict.
Forgetting to use 'this' when needed, causing parameter values to not be assigned to instance variables.
Trying to use 'this' in static methods (where no instance exists).
